description: An hourly cron + /autopilot skill that runs the Research→Plan→Implement→Audit→Improve loop unattended: selects the next harness-infra item (curated backlog → throttled /harness-audit fallback → slug dedupe), builds it via pm decompose + /ship-spec + /delegate, and finalizes a ready-for-review PR with green CI. Guardrails: cap 2 open autopilot PRs, never auto-merge, harness-infra scope only. Critic-gated; 7 HIGH findings resolved in-place.

userStories.title: Seed the curated backlog
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want a curated crons/autopilot-backlog.md checklist so that I can steer what autopilot builds without touching code.
acceptanceCriteria: crons/autopilot-backlog.md exists as plain markdown with a prominent top comment stating it is a reference file, NOT a cron definition (runtime must never schedule it), a short format/usage header, and an '## Active items' checklist
File contains no 'schedule:' field; grep -c '^---$' crons/autopilot-backlog.md returns 0
Each item carries an explicit deterministic slug token for dedupe (e.g. '- [ ] drift-sentinel — ...')
Seeded first item is the Drift Sentinel work (slug drift-sentinel) with '(source: memory/MEMORY.md 2026-06-10)'
grep '- \[ \]' crons/autopilot-backlog.md returns the Drift Sentinel line
userStories.notes: No deps. Format precedent: crons/heartbeat.md '## Active items'.

userStories.title: Hourly autopilot cron definition
userStories.description: As the runtime, I want a crons/autopilot.md cron so that the loop fires every hour automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontmatter: id: autopilot, schedule: "5 * * * *" (offset 5 min from heartbeat's 0 * * * * to avoid a concurrent memory-log write race), timezone: America/Denver, enabled: true, overlap: false, catchup: false, one-line description
Body is thin: invokes the /autopilot skill plus a one-line guardrail reminder (cap-2, no auto-merge, harness-infra only); does NOT replicate the skill procedure
awk '/^---$/{f=!f; next} f{print}' crons/autopilot.md emits all required fields
crons/heartbeat.md is unchanged (git diff --quiet crons/heartbeat.md)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No deps. Cron shape precedent: crons/heartbeat.md.

userStories.title: The /autopilot loop skill
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I want a .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md so that the loop procedure is version-controlled, testable on demand, and reusable.
acceptanceCriteria: File exists with valid frontmatter (grep -c '^---$' == 2) including name, description, and argument-hint: [--dry-run]
--dry-run mode prints the selected item + open-autopilot-PR count, then exits without calling /ship-spec or /delegate

Six ordered sections: (1) guardrail pre-check — exit if gh pr list --label autopilot --state open >= 2 (log SKIPPED-CAP2 + liveness, do NOT run /harness-audit), require clean tree and branch development; (2) select item — backlog top-unchecked else throttled /harness-audit fallback (skip audit if a today autopilot-audit memory entry is <6h old), map selection to a deterministic kebab slug, dedupe (skip + log SKIPPED-DEDUPE if an open issue/PR title or branch contains that slug), and if both sources exhausted log NOTHING-TO-BUILD + liveness and exit 0 without touching git/GitHub; (3) pm decompose via Agent subagent_type: pm; (4) /ship-spec then add 'autopilot' label to the PR; (5) /delegate run to completion in-process (NO run_in_background; await all workers before finalize); (6) finalize — push, mark ready via gh pr ready ONLY if /ci-status is green, else leave draft + comment, then git checkout development to restore the guardrail branch
Failure compensation: if /ship-spec opened an issue but a later stage fails, comment on the issue/PR that autopilot aborted mid-run and leave it draft (non-destructive); log DELEGATE-FAIL
Halt visibility: a /ship-spec critic-gate HALT writes a distinct Result: HALT-CRITIC-GATE memory entry + HALT-CRITIC-GATE liveness line
Ensures the 'autopilot' GitHub label exists (idempotent create-if-missing) before the cap-2 query

Memory-log block (skill-lint shape) AND a crons/.cron.log liveness line copying the printf FORMAT from crons/heartbeat.md with autopilot-specific status tokens: OK, SKIPPED-CAP2, SKIPPED-DEDUPE, NOTHING-TO-BUILD, PR-READY, PR-DRAFT-CI-RED, HALT-CRITIC-GATE, DELEGATE-FAIL
States the scope guard 'harness-infra only — never sandbox application code' explicitly; no auto-merge instruction anywhere
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Deps US-001, US-002. Precedents: health-check/SKILL.md, skill-lint/SKILL.md; heartbeat.md closing liveness line.

userStories.title: Register /autopilot in CLAUDE.md Skills table
userStories.description: As a reader of CLAUDE.md, I want an /autopilot row in the Skills table so that the skill is discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Exactly one new row in the CLAUDE.md Skills table; the 'When' cell is the first sentence of the SKILL.md description (matching existing rows)
git diff CLAUDE.md shows a single added row and no other changed hunks
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document the cron + backlog in crons/README.md
userStories.description: As a reader of crons/README.md, I want the autopilot cron and backlog documented so that their purpose and lifecycle are clear.
acceptanceCriteria: The Scheduled-jobs table gains a row for autopilot.md (schedule 5 * * * *, description, kill-switch note enabled: false)
autopilot-backlog.md is documented in prose as a reference file (curated backlog) explicitly NOT a scheduled job — must not appear in the Scheduled-jobs table
grep -c 'autopilot' crons/README.md >= 2
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Changelog [Unreleased] entry
userStories.description: As the release process, I want a [Unreleased] changelog entry so that the feature is captured.
acceptanceCriteria: One imperative bullet under '## [Unreleased]' -> '### Added', linking issue #4 / the PR
No versioned section modified
Typecheck passes
